name: Aloha Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 Chicken Bouillon cubes; Borden Low Sodium
recipeInstructions:
1. Simmer the chicken in water with bouillon cubes until cooked through.
2. Remove meat from bones and cut into chunks. Reserve 1 cup of the cooking liquid.
3. In a frying pan or wok, melt margarine over medium heat.
4. Add radishes, green peppers, and pineapple to the pan. Cook until crisp-tender but not brown.
5. (While the chicken is cooking, prepare the rice.)
6. Mix 1 cup of reserved chicken broth with 1/2 cup pineapple juice and 1 teaspoon soy sauce in a bowl.
7. Dissolve 2 tablespoons flour in 1 tablespoon cold water. Stir to remove lumps.
8. Add the flour mixture to the pan with vegetables, stirring well.
9. Incorporate the cooked chicken pieces and season with salt.
10. Cook until all ingredients are heated through.
11. Serve hot over cooked rice. Optionally, sprinkle chow mein noodles on top.
12. Serves 6 (Portion: 3/4 cup mixture served over 3/4 cup cooked rice)
Nutrients per serving: Calories 320, fat 7g, cholesterol 58mg, carbohydrate 39g, sodium 314mg.
Exchanges: Bread 2 1/2, Meat 2.
